data.title: U.S. Consumer Sentiment Falls Amid Concerns Over Gasoline Prices
data.description: U.S. consumer sentiment has dropped to its lowest level in three months, reflecting growing concerns about gasoline prices. Bloomberg posted on X that the ongoing conflict with Iran has heightened fears regarding the potential impact on fuel costs. This decline in sentiment underscores the economic uncertainty facing consumers as geopolitical tensions continue to influence market dynamics. The situation remains fluid, with analysts closely monitoring developments that could further affect consumer confidence and economic stability.

data.title: Iran Denies Special Oil Access to India Amid Strait of Hormuz Blockade
data.description: Iran has refuted claims that it granted India exclusive oil access through the Strait of Hormuz, a crucial passage for nearly 20% of the world's oil supply. According to NS3.AI, Iranian authorities clarified that no agreement was reached with India, emphasizing that the blockade remains in effect for all nations. The restriction will continue until the United States and Israel withdraw from the ongoing conflict.

data.title: Ethereum(ETH) Surpasses 2,200 USDT with a 6.15% Increase in 24 Hours
data.description: On Mar 13, 2026, 14:20 PM(UTC). According to Binance Market Data, Ethereum has crossed the 2,200 USDT benchmark and is now trading at 2,203.100098 USDT, with a narrowed 6.15% increase in 24 hours.

data.title: U.S. Investment-Grade Bond Sales Near Record Levels Amid Global Tensions
data.description: Despite ongoing geopolitical tensions in the Middle East, increasing yields, and rising oil prices, corporate borrowers in the United States have shown resilience. Bloomberg posted on X that U.S. investment-grade bond sales have approached near-record levels this week. The robust activity in the bond market indicates strong demand and confidence among investors, even as private credit markets face distress. This trend highlights the ability of corporate borrowers to navigate challenging economic conditions while maintaining access to capital.

data.title: Australian Dollar Strengthens Amid Oil Price Surge and Rate Hike Expectations
data.description: Mitsubishi UFJ analyst Derek Halpenny has indicated that the anticipated rise in oil prices due to the Iran conflict may lead some central banks to increase interest rates. According to Odaily, Halpenny noted that such rate hikes would benefit currencies of economies with resilient growth. He stated, "Raising interest rates in weak economic conditions is generally seen as negative for currencies." Amid expectations of further rate hikes and potential trade improvements from rising energy prices, the Australian dollar has emerged as one of the top-performing currencies among the G10 nations. LSEG data reveals that the market currently estimates an 80% probability of the Reserve Bank of Australia raising interest rates by 25 basis points on Tuesday.

data.title: Binance Futures to Delist Multiple Perpetual Contracts in March 2026
data.description: Binance Futures has announced plans to delist several USDⓈ-M and COIN-M perpetual contracts on March 17 and March 18, 2026. According to BWEnews, this decision is part of Binance's regular review process to ensure the quality and performance of its offerings. The delisting will affect a range of contracts, and users are advised to manage their positions accordingly before the specified dates to avoid potential disruptions. Binance emphasizes its commitment to providing a secure and efficient trading environment for its users. Further details on the specific contracts to be delisted will be provided by Binance in due course.

data.title: Bank of America: Oil price shocks may push up Fed inflation forecasts; Powell will emphasize a wait-and-see approach.
data.description: Bank of America stated that the Federal Reserve will have to deal with another supply shock—soaring oil prices. In the Summary of Economic Projections (SEP) released ahead of the March Fed meeting, both overall and core inflation forecasts are likely to be revised upwards. The Bank of America report stated, "If long-term growth expectations are also revised upwards, we expect the median dot plot of long-term interest rates to likely shift slightly upwards." In this scenario, the bank believes Fed Chairman Powell may acknowledge the risks of stagflation while emphasizing a wait-and-see approach. (Jinshi)

data.title: EU Proposes New Measures to Enhance Business Competitiveness
data.description:
The European Union is addressing its competitiveness challenges with a new proposal aimed at assisting businesses in navigating the single market. Bloomberg posted on X, highlighting the EU's efforts to bolster economic performance amid growing global competition. The proposal seeks to streamline regulations and reduce barriers for companies operating within the EU, fostering a more conducive environment for business growth.

This initiative comes as the EU faces increasing pressure to maintain its economic standing against other major economies. By simplifying market access and enhancing regulatory frameworks, the EU aims to attract more investments and support innovation across its member states.

The proposal is part of a broader strategy to ensure the EU remains a competitive player on the global stage, addressing concerns from businesses about the complexities of operating within the single market. The EU's commitment to improving its economic landscape is seen as a crucial step in sustaining growth and prosperity in the region.

data.title: U.S. President Trump Comments on Iranian Leader's Condition
data.description: U.S. President Donald Trump stated that he believes the Iranian leader is still alive but has been injured. According to Jin10, President Trump made these remarks amid ongoing tensions between the United States and Iran. The situation has drawn international attention, with various geopolitical implications being considered by analysts. The President's comments come at a time when diplomatic relations are strained, and the global community is closely monitoring developments in the region.

data.title: Eurogroup President Pielakakis: Greek Economy to Maintain 2% Growth Even in Worst Case
data.description: Eurogroup President Pielakakis stated that Greece's economic growth rate is expected to remain at 2% even in the worst-case scenario. According to Jin10, recent measures to cap profits on fuel and food are not anticipated to have a fiscal impact on Greece's budget. The statement underscores the resilience of the Greek economy amid ongoing challenges.

data.title: Peloton's Strategic Shift Under Former Apple Executive Peter Stern
data.description: Peloton is undergoing a strategic transformation under the leadership of former Apple executive Peter Stern. Bloomberg posted on X that Stern is focusing on revitalizing the company by emphasizing treadmills, enhancing subscription services, and implementing innovative marketing strategies. This move aims to reposition Peloton in the competitive fitness market, leveraging Stern's expertise to drive growth and sustainability.

data.title: Carvana Announces 5-for-1 Stock Split to Lower Share Price
data.description: Carvana's board has approved a 5-for-1 stock split to reduce the automotive retailer's high share price. Bloomberg posted on X, highlighting that this decision follows a significant multiyear rally from the pandemic's impact. The stock split aims to make shares more accessible to a broader range of investors, reflecting the company's strong performance and growth in recent years.

data.title: Solana Stablecoin Trading Volume Hits Record $650 Billion in February
data.description: Solana-based stablecoin trading volume reached an unprecedented $650 billion in February. According to NS3.AI, this surge was driven by heightened demand for on-chain payments. The increase highlights the growing interest in utilizing stablecoins for transactions within the Solana ecosystem, reflecting broader trends in the cryptocurrency market.

data.title: UK Prime Minister Starmer Vows Government Intervention Against Fuel Companies Exploiting Customers
data.description: UK Prime Minister Keir Starmer has announced that his government will take action if fuel companies attempt to exploit customers. According to Jin10, Starmer emphasized the importance of protecting consumers from unfair practices in the energy sector. He stated that the government is prepared to intervene to ensure that fuel companies do not take advantage of customers, particularly during times of economic uncertainty. This announcement comes amid growing concerns about rising fuel prices and their impact on households. Starmer's commitment to consumer protection highlights the government's focus on addressing economic challenges and ensuring fair practices in the market.

data.title: 6.5 Magnitude Earthquake Strikes Off Chile's Coast
data.description: A 6.5 magnitude earthquake occurred off the coast of Chile's Atacama region on March 13, according to the Euro-Mediterranean Seismological Center. The earthquake struck at 10:39 local time with a depth of 22 kilometers. According to Jin10, there have been no tsunami warnings issued at this time.

data.title: OPEC Increases Oil Production as Saudi Arabia Boosts Output
data.description: OPEC's crude oil production saw an increase last month, driven by Saudi Arabia's decision to enhance output amid escalating regional tensions. Bloomberg posted on X, highlighting the findings of a recent survey. The rise in production comes as geopolitical factors continue to influence the oil market dynamics.

data.title: UBS Asset Management Anticipates Market Overreaction to Interest Rate Hike Expectations
data.description:
UBS Asset Management is expressing caution regarding the market's current stance on anticipated interest rate hikes by the Bank of England and the European Central Bank. Bloomberg posted on X, highlighting UBS AM's belief that investors may have overestimated the likelihood and impact of these potential monetary policy changes. The firm suggests that the market's aggressive positioning could lead to volatility if the central banks' actions do not align with expectations.

UBS AM's analysis comes amid ongoing discussions about inflation and economic recovery in Europe, with central banks weighing their options to manage these challenges. The firm advises investors to consider the broader economic context and potential risks associated with the current market sentiment.

As the Bank of England and the European Central Bank continue to assess their strategies, UBS AM remains vigilant, monitoring developments closely to adjust its investment approach accordingly. The firm emphasizes the importance of staying informed and adaptable in the face of evolving economic conditions.

data.title: STOCKS | Russell 2000 Index Rises Over 1%
data.description: The Russell 2000 Index, a key benchmark for small-cap stocks in the United States, experienced a significant increase of over 1%. According to Jin10, this rise reflects a positive shift in market sentiment, as investors show renewed interest in smaller companies. The upward movement in the index suggests a broader confidence in the economic outlook, despite ongoing uncertainties in the global market. Analysts are closely monitoring this trend to assess its potential impact on future investment strategies and market dynamics.

data.title: Crypto stocks opened higher across the board, with Circle rising 3.49%.
data.description: According to data from msx.com, US stocks opened higher, with the three major indices rising: the Dow Jones Industrial Average up 0.47%, the S&P 500 up 0.5%, and the Nasdaq Composite up 0.49%. Cryptocurrency stocks generally rose, with Circle up 3.49%, COIN up 3.35%, DFDV up 8.09%, MSTR up 4.89%, and SBET up 5.48%. msx.com is a decentralized RWA trading platform that has listed hundreds of RWA tokens, covering US stocks and ETF tokens such as AAPL, AMZN, GOOGL, META, MSFT, NFLX, and NVDA.

data.title: STOCKS | U.S. Stock Market Opens Higher as Adobe CEO Resigns
data.description: The U.S. stock market opened with gains across its major indices. According to Jin10, the Dow Jones Industrial Average rose by 0.47%, the S&P 500 increased by 0.5%, and the Nasdaq Composite climbed by 0.49%. In corporate news, Adobe experienced a significant drop of 7% following the announcement that its CEO, who has led the company for 18 years, will be stepping down.
